attacking_style: Specializes in breakaway wins rather than solo attacks from the peloton. When attacking from breaks, prefers final 500m-1.5km on uphill finishes with perfectly timed accelerations, demonstrating superior finish speed management.
team_tactics: Operates primarily as a breakaway opportunist with minimal visible team setup. Relies on personal positioning and timing within established breaks rather than domestique-led attacks.
terrain_preference: Moderate uphill finishes and sustained high-pace days suit him best, showing superior freshness management. Severely exposed on gradients exceeding 15% where he loses contact with lead groups.
vulnerability: Steep gradients (15%+) where power-to-weight becomes critical. Historically struggled to convert breakaway presence into wins (Tour de France 'nearly-man syndrome') though this improved from 2022 onward.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist: 5 of 5 wins came from established breaks, never from peloton attacks.
Late accelerations: prefers attacking with 500m-1.5km remaining rather than early moves.
Sustained Vuelta form: multiple wins across 2019, 2022, 2023 editions showing consistent performance.
Superior freshness after long efforts: repeatedly demonstrates better condition than break companions on finale.
Struggles on extreme gradients: lost contact when gradients hit 15%, prefers moderate climbs.
form_trajectory: positive: Three wins across 2022-2023 Vuelta editions with sustained form between stage wins shows marked improvement from earlier 'nearly-man' status. Confirmed ability to animate and win from breaks consistently.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
